title: P2-006: Run held-out planner acceptance and trace one revision
description:
## Context
P2-001 through P2-005 provide the measurable generation and review loop. Phase 2 exits only with recorded evidence from the ten held-out briefs and one review-driven second iteration.

## Task
Run the configured live provider across all ten held-out briefs without hand-editing outputs, preserve raw attempts and reports, measure validity, review representative candidate comparisons, and trace one revision from an observed defect to a prompt/plan/mapping change and comparable rerun.

## Deliverables
- Durable acceptance run covering all ten held-out cases with model/prompt/config metadata and secret-safe raw attempts.
- Aggregate report against the 8/10 valid-without-hand-editing target.
- At least one reviewed multi-candidate comparison.
- One evidence-linked revision and comparable second run, with defect attribution.
- Final Phase 2 status/handoff documentation.

## Boundaries
Do not hand-edit model outputs to improve the metric. Do not weaken validators or scenario expectations. If credentials/provider availability block a live run, retain the issue open and report the blocker rather than fabricating evidence.
acceptance_criteria: A durable live-provider acceptance run shows at least 8 of 10 held-out briefs yielding valid plans without hand editing, or the issue remains open with truthful failure evidence. Invalid output never renders. At least one candidate preference and one evidence-linked revision are recorded. npm run check and documented end-to-end commands pass; no secret appears in artifacts or logs.
notes: Implementation and live evidence in progress. Added hardened planner-acceptance-run.v1 CLI/artifacts; pinned gpt-5.4-mini-2026-03-17 baseline 20260715T032337.085Z-a90bb7434449 scored 0/10 because prompts referenced but did not supply the schema. Evidence-linked revision supplies authoritative schema plus exact scenario expectations to initial/repair prompts; comparable run 20260715T033040.807Z-5c2dabdf6e27 scored 8/10 without hand edits (5 first-pass, 3 repaired; all 10 schema-valid, two retained warning failures). Distinct qualified-answer candidate set 20260715T033357.378Z-804f4bd16ff8 created. Human feedback found candidates visually close, silence unexplained, and rubric unanchored; guided finalized comparison 20260715T072026.184Z-0a5567c07680 now explains no audio, summarizes exact pair differences, anchors every 1/3/5 rubric dimension, provides readable pair-level checks, and supports explicit score copying. Automated checks and direct Chrome/offline/copy verification are green; awaiting the evidence-bound human preference before final trace and closure.

title: P2-005: Build side-by-side SVG review and preference artifacts
description:
## Context
P2-004 preserves multiple valid plans and semantic diffs. Reviewers need to compare their deterministic local SVG renders and record a preference tied to concrete evidence.

## Task
Create a self-contained side-by-side local review artifact for selected candidates, reuse the existing renderer/run contracts, and persist rubric scores, preference, observations, and defect attribution without network dependencies.

## Deliverables
- Self-contained comparison HTML linking or embedding deterministic candidate players and semantic diff context.
- Responsive synchronized playback controls suitable for full size and approximately 320px-per-candidate review.
- Versioned preference/review record tied to candidate, plan, rig, render-script, and prompt/provider metadata checksums.
- CLI to create a comparison bundle from a candidate set.
- Automated generation/security tests plus direct Chrome functional review.

## Boundaries
Do not add automatic vision scoring or an LLM judge. Human preference is explicit and may be absent until review. Keep full-duration AI-delegate disclosure.
acceptance_criteria: A reviewer can open a self-contained file:// comparison, synchronously play/restart selected candidates, inspect their semantic diff, and record a traceable preference/rubric. Disclosure and exact neutral reset remain visible/correct. The bundle is checksummed and non-overwriting. Tests pass and Chrome review covers full and constrained widths with no external resource requests.
notes: Implemented deterministic, non-overwriting local-SVG comparison bundles for two checksum-verified successful candidates, with nested finalized render runs, semantic diff, sandboxed synchronized file:// players, responsive human rubric/preference export, exact evidence bindings, path/symlink containment, and fail-closed completed-review validation. Review fixes included iframe load gating, hidden embedded controls, strict offline CSP, observation validation, and rendering from the exact validated/copied plan bytes to close the evidence-binding TOCTOU gap. Validation: npm run check (105/105), npm run demo:dry, fresh planner:compare finalized successfully, git diff --check, and direct browser review at desktop, approximately 320px columns, and stacked widths with synchronized start/pause/restart, disclosure/neutral lifecycle, complete/partial export behavior, and no external requests or console/network errors.
close_reason: Accepted: offline side-by-side SVG comparison bundles and evidence-bound human review artifacts implemented and verified.

title: P2-004: Generate candidate sets and semantic plan diffs
description:
## Context
P2-003 can produce one validated plan with provenance. Review requires multiple bounded alternatives and an inspectable semantic comparison rather than opaque prompt drift.

## Task
Generate a bounded candidate set for one scenario, assign stable candidate identities, preserve provider/attempt provenance, and produce deterministic semantic diffs focused on states, gestures, speech acts, timing, attention, affect, and disclosure.

## Deliverables
- Candidate-set manifest and non-overwriting artifact folder.
- Bounded sequential or concurrency-safe candidate generation with cancellation.
- Deterministic machine-readable and human-readable semantic plan diff.
- CLI for generating/evaluating a candidate set and diffing selected candidates.
- Tests for stable IDs/order, duplicate candidates, partial provider failure, cancellation, and meaningful diff output.

## Boundaries
Do not rank candidates with another model and do not change runtime/rig behavior. Candidate identity must not depend on secrets or nondeterministic object ordering.
acceptance_criteria: One command can preserve multiple validated candidates for a corpus brief with stable manifests and provenance. Pairwise semantic diffs identify meaningful plan-level changes without renderer IDs or raw frame data. Duplicate and partial-failure behavior is explicit. Artifacts never overwrite prior sets. npm run check passes offline using a fake provider.
notes: Implemented bounded sequential 2-5 candidate generation with stable content-derived IDs, explicit duplicates/partial failures/cancellation, non-overwriting checksummed artifacts and exact sanitized-provider provenance. Added deterministic JSON/Markdown semantic diffs with ID-independent LCS event alignment, checksum-verified candidate loading and path/symlink containment, plus opt-in generation and offline diff CLIs. Review fixes include SIGINT/SIGTERM cancellation finalization and explicit provider sanitization contract. Validation: npm run check (96/96), npm run demo:dry, git diff --check.
close_reason: Accepted: durable candidate sets and meaningful renderer-independent semantic diffs implemented and verified offline.

title: P2-002: Add provider-neutral planner interface and OpenAI adapter
description:
## Context
P2-001 defines the evaluation contract that generated plans must satisfy. The project now needs one production-shaped provider boundary without coupling providers to rendering or making offline checks depend on secrets.

## Task
Define a provider-neutral planner request/response interface and implement one OpenAI Responses API adapter using environment-based credentials and model selection. Preserve prompt/version/model/request metadata and raw provider output for later extraction and audit.

## Deliverables
- Provider-neutral planner types and orchestration seam.
- OpenAI Responses API adapter with explicit timeout/cancellation and actionable errors.
- Mock-HTTP tests for success, API errors, malformed responses, timeout/cancellation, and secret-safe diagnostics.
- CLI or library entry point that can generate raw candidate output for one corpus brief.
- Configuration and live-smoke documentation; offline checks require no key.

## Boundaries
Do not parse/repair model JSON beyond extracting the provider returned text in this issue. Do not invoke the renderer. Never log or persist API keys.
acceptance_criteria: The interface can be exercised with a fake provider and the OpenAI adapter. Raw output and non-secret request metadata are preserved. Timeout, cancellation, HTTP/API errors, and missing configuration fail explicitly without leaking secrets. Tests use mocks and npm run check works without credentials. A documented opt-in live smoke command exists.
notes: Implemented provider-neutral raw planner contracts, deterministic prompt rendering/version identity, direct-fetch OpenAI Responses API adapter, and opt-in raw smoke CLI. Independent review found and fixed post-header body-read abort classification and enumerable credential storage; adapter now uses ECMAScript private fields, classifies fetch/body timeout vs caller cancellation, redacts/bounds errors, validates X-Client-Request-Id, preserves non-secret provenance, and rejects malformed/failed/incomplete/ambiguous outputs. Verification: npm run check (75/75), targeted stream-abort/redaction/config tests, keyless CLI fails with empty stdout, npm run demo:dry, git diff --check. Live call intentionally deferred to held-out acceptance.
close_reason: Provider-neutral raw planner boundary, prompt versioning, hardened OpenAI Responses adapter, mock coverage, and opt-in live smoke CLI are implemented and verified offline.

title: P2-003: Implement bounded extraction, validation, and repair
description:
## Context
P2-002 yields raw provider text; existing validators can assess animation plans. Untrusted model output must be extracted, validated, and repaired within strict bounds before rendering.

## Task
Implement a deterministic planner orchestration loop that extracts one JSON object, validates it structurally and semantically, requests bounded repair when needed, and fails closed after a configured maximum attempt count.

## Deliverables
- Strict JSON extraction with explicit ambiguity/trailing-content failures.
- Validation result fed back as bounded, structured repair context.
- Configurable low default maximum attempts with full attempt provenance.
- Final typed success/failure result that cannot expose an invalid plan as renderable.
- Tests covering valid first pass, fenced/noisy output policy, schema and semantic repair, repeated invalid output, cancellation, and provider failure.

## Boundaries
Do not silently mutate model plans in application code. Do not tune renderer behavior or add candidate ranking yet.
acceptance_criteria: A valid first response returns a typed validated plan. Invalid responses either become valid through the bounded provider repair call or return a terminal failure with every raw output and diagnostic retained. No invalid plan can cross the success type boundary. Attempt limits, cancellation, and errors are tested deterministically; npm run check passes offline.
notes: Implemented strict whole-text object extraction, warning-free scenario validation, typed success/failure boundary, default initial+one bounded repair, cancellation, and complete attempt provenance. Review prompted exact per-attempt prompt capture, shared orchestration IDs with unique hashed provider request IDs, bounded policy/diagnostic repair budgets, and a post-provider cancellation check. Verification: npm run check (86/86), adversarial extraction/policy/repair/cancellation/provenance tests, keyless planner:generate returns machine-readable nonzero failure, npm run demo:dry, git diff --check. No invalid plan is exposed on failure.
close_reason: Bounded extraction, scenario validation, repair orchestration, typed fail-closed results, and audited attempt provenance are implemented and verified.

title: Phase 2: Measurable planner iteration loop
description:
## Objective
Build a provider-neutral, reviewable planner loop in which high-level scene briefs produce bounded animation-plan.v1 candidates, invalid output fails closed, multiple candidates can be compared through the existing deterministic local SVG renderer, and preference/revision evidence is durable.

## Verification scope
This parent is an integration and acceptance task. Child issues own the implementation slices: evaluation contract/corpus, provider adapter, extraction/repair, candidate diffing, side-by-side review, and held-out acceptance.

## Boundaries
Keep model-provider code separate from the renderer. The planner emits semantic intent, never renderer IDs or frame curves. Offline tests and replay remain usable without an API key. Do not add voice, Teams, transcript ingestion, RAG, or VTube work.
acceptance_criteria: All six child slices are closed and integrated. At least 8 of 10 held-out briefs produce valid plans without hand editing in a recorded provider run. Invalid outputs never reach rendering. Multiple candidates and a review-driven second iteration are traceable through durable artifacts. npm run check and the Phase 2 end-to-end commands pass.

title: P2-001: Define planner evaluation contract and held-out corpus
description:
## Context
The repository has a planner prompt, animation-plan schema/diagnostics, and ten prose scenario briefs, but no machine-readable evaluation contract. This must exist before choosing or tuning a model provider.

## Task
Create a versioned provider-independent scenario corpus and deterministic CLI/report for evaluating candidate animation-plan.v1 files against schema, semantic diagnostics, and scenario-specific policy expectations.

## Deliverables
- Machine-readable fixtures for all ten existing scenario briefs, with required duration/rig and scenario-specific semantic expectations.
- Deterministic evaluator library and CLI that accepts candidate plans without network access.
- Stable JSON report with per-scenario structural, semantic, and policy checks plus aggregate valid-plan metrics.
- Known-good and intentionally failing candidate fixtures and focused tests.
- Scripts/docs for the offline evaluation command.

## Boundaries
Do not add a model provider, network call, repair loop, renderer change, or subjective visual scoring.
acceptance_criteria: All ten briefs are versioned as machine-readable cases. The evaluator reports deterministic pass/fail reasons for schema errors, semantic errors, disclosure/reset violations, authority/deferral expectations, and scenario-specific constraints. Good and bad fixtures prove behavior. Reports are byte-stable for identical inputs. npm run check and the documented offline evaluation command pass without an API key or network.
notes: Implemented animation-plan-corpus.v1 with all ten held-out briefs, runtime schema and semantic corpus validation, deterministic provider-free evaluation, strict manifest confinement, scenario policy checks, exact report serialization, good/failing fixtures, CLI/scripts, and docs. Independent review found and prompted fixes for malformed-corpus denominator drift, token-duration authority checks, out-of-context speech, reset-gesture bypass, and manifest traversal. Verification: npm run check (62/62), good evaluation 10/10, failing evaluation 0/10 as exit-0 report data, repeated output byte-identical, npm run demo:dry, git diff --check.
close_reason: Provider-independent ten-scenario planner evaluation contract, deterministic CLI/report, adversarial fixtures, and strict fail-closed configuration validation are implemented and verified.

title: M1-SVG-003: Generate a self-contained offline SVG player
description:
Generate a single local HTML file containing the first-party stylized SVG avatar, compiled render script, rig metadata, playback controls, countdown, and full-duration AI-delegate disclosure. The file must open directly without a server, network, CDN, external application, or licensed model asset.

The player is a dumb playback surface: runtime timing/easing stays in the compiler; SVG-specific IDs and transforms stay in the renderer/profile.
acceptance_criteria: One CLI command validates plan/profile and emits a self-contained player.html. Opening it from file:// in Chrome/Safari plays the 20–45 second plan with start/pause/restart and visible current-state/time diagnostics. Full-duration disclosure is rendered in-frame. The avatar returns to byte/visually stable neutral after completion and restart. Tests verify generated content, escaping, embedded data, no external URLs/fetches, and deterministic output. npm run check passes and a real local-browser manual test is documented.
notes: Implemented strict local-SVG frame resolution and deterministic self-contained HTML/SVG generation with embedded render script, precomputed frames, full disclosure, Start/3-second countdown, Pause/Resume, Restart, semantic state/time diagnostics, CSP/offline safeguards, escaping, and exact neutral lifecycle. Automated validation on 2026-07-14: npm run check passed (42/42 tests); dry/mapping/render-script/player demos passed; generated player was 329,019 bytes with 561 frames and no external/network references; git diff --check passed. Manual Chrome 150 file:// check passed at full and 320px width: initial neutral/Start, countdown pause/resume, restart neutral, 28-second completion neutral, semantic state diagnostics, no page errors, and no resource requests. Safari and taste review remain pending.
close_reason: Self-contained offline SVG player generation and the required functional local-browser test are implemented, documented, and verified.

title: M1-SVG-001: Define the local SVG rig profile and avatar vocabulary
description:
Extend rig-profile.v1 with a discriminated local_svg renderer branch and a first-party stylized avatar profile. Preserve the existing VTube branch unchanged. Map renderer-independent abstract controls and gestures to bounded SVG pose/expression directives; no renderer IDs may enter animation-plan.v1.

Keep the avatar vocabulary deliberately small: head x/y/z, gaze, brows, eyes/blink, mouth/smile/caveat, speaking-ready, deferral/boundary, and neutral reset.
acceptance_criteria: RigProfile is a discriminated union keyed by renderer. JSON Schema validates both unchanged vtube_studio profiles and local_svg profiles while rejecting mixed/impossible shapes. A committed first-party local SVG profile matches the reference plan targetRig and maps every used state/gesture. Structural and semantic validator tests cover both branches, missing mappings, ranges, and neutral values. npm run check and mapping inspection pass without network or external apps.
notes: Implemented a strict RigProfile discriminated union preserving vtube_studio and adding local_svg; added a first-party bounded local SVG profile, renderer-neutral abstract pose mapping, separate SVG/VTube resolution, intensity scaling, and negative compatibility tests. Validation on 2026-07-14: npm run check passed (23/23 tests), npm run demo:dry passed, npm run demo:mapping passed, and git diff --check passed.
close_reason: Local SVG rig contract, first-party profile, bounded mapping, and compatibility validation are implemented and verified.

title: M1-SVG-002: Compile a deterministic abstract render script
description:
Implement the renderer-independent runtime core needed by both SVG now and VTube later. Compile validated timeline events and abstract controls into a fixed-tick render script with monotonic plan time, deterministic ordering, bounded easing, gesture lifecycle, conflict resolution, cancellation markers, and final neutral reset.

The script must contain semantic/abstract resolved values, not SVG element IDs or VTube parameter IDs.
acceptance_criteria: A documented fixed tick rate and easing function produce byte-identical render-script JSONL for the same plan/profile. Same-time events have deterministic priority. Parameter values are clamped and have one owner; gesture collisions are rejected or coalesced deliberately. State gaps, cancellation, completion, and recoverable error paths yield explicit final reset/release records. Fake-clock and golden-output tests cover interpolation, stillness, ordering, duplicate gestures, and reset. npm run check passes.
notes: Implemented pure render-script.v1 compilation at 50 ms fixed ticks with smoothstep transitions, four-decimal quantization, renderer-neutral parameter/pose channels, exact marker ordering, neutral gap handling, same-name pose conflict policy, cancellation/error reset ramps, and explicit end/reset/release. Validation on 2026-07-14: npm run check passed (35/35 tests); dry-run and mapping demos passed; machine-readable demo:render-script produced 595 parseable records / 561 on-grid frames with no renderer IDs; git diff --check passed.
close_reason: Deterministic abstract render-script compiler, JSONL CLI, lifecycle/conflict semantics, tests, and documentation are implemented and verified.

title: M1-SVG-000: Adopt a policy-safe local SVG renderer
description:
## Decision Needed

Replace VTube Studio as the first Milestone 1 renderer because organizational IT policy prevents installation. Preserve the semantic plan/runtime/rig boundaries and retain VTube as a deferred future adapter.

## Rationale

A first-party local SVG player can exercise visual legibility, deterministic timing, stillness, disclosure, and review artifacts without external applications, proprietary runtimes, model licensing, network access, or policy bypass. The existing abstract-command mapping already provides the renderer-independent seam.

## Alternatives Considered

1. Browser SVG/Canvas renderer: first-party, offline, deterministic, and dependency-light. Recommended.
2. Browser Live2D/Cubism: rejected for Milestone 1 because it reintroduces proprietary runtime/model licensing and likely policy risk.
3. Wait for VTube approval: rejected because it leaves the core visual hypothesis untested indefinitely.

## Non-Goals

Do not delete the VTube scaffold, weaken disclosure, add voice/Teams/OBS, or turn the SVG adapter into a general graphics framework.
acceptance_criteria: ADR 0005 records the policy constraint and accepted local SVG decision; ADR 0001 and ADR 0004 are marked deferred/superseded for Milestone 1 without deleting their rationale. STATUS, architecture, milestone, roadmap, and setup docs truthfully distinguish the SVG milestone path from deferred VTube work. The epic and Beads graph point at the policy-safe critical path, and npm run check remains green.
notes: Implemented ADR 0005 and updated current routing/status/architecture/milestone/setup documents. VTube rationale and scaffold are retained as deferred reference with explicit policy-safe resumption criteria. Validation on 2026-07-14: npm run check passed (16/16 tests), npm run demo:dry passed, and git diff --check passed.
close_reason: ADR 0005 accepted and project/agent documentation now routes Milestone 1 through the policy-safe local SVG path.

title: M1-009: Record, review, and replay the first comparable animation
description:
Exercise the complete policy-safe Milestone 1 loop in the self-contained local SVG player, review the 20–45 second reference scene at full and meeting-tile size, attribute defects, revise, and perform a comparable second replay.

This is the visual proof; dry-run, mapping inspection, or generated files alone are not completion.
acceptance_criteria: The unchanged semantic reference plan is replayed in the generated local SVG player with visible full-duration offline AI-delegate disclosure. The run bundle includes player, deterministic script, manifest, profile/plan copies, notes, and optional recording. The rubric is completed at full and approximately 320px width; at least five changes are attributed to plan/runtime/rig/renderer/capture. A second replay starts/ends neutral and produces the same deterministic script. The six target communicative states and deferral boundary are identifiable; repository checks pass.
close_reason: Completed the policy-safe local SVG review/replay loop. Replay A (20260715T001338.814Z-79518f1791c2) and final Replay B (20260715T003121.821Z-d97aaab3251d) retain byte-identical plan and render-script inputs (plan SHA bff27295694c1a212c25573bde0dba61166835f63d1b082f37ebdb59ebc2854a; script SHA b2e8e1673a8b0043c375b2d1828b48eed22d7a069ea2871d16b0e847589c2eed). Rig/renderer-only revisions improved 320px legibility and responsive controls. Full-size and 320px Chrome playback passed countdown, pause/restart, completion, persistent disclosure, zero external resource requests, and exact neutral reset. Both durable bundles have checksummed manifests and completed review notes; optional recording fields remain null. npm run check, demo:dry, demo:mapping, demo:render-script, and demo:player pass. VTube remains explicitly untested/deferred because organizational IT blocks installation.

title: M1-008: Persist durable playback run artifacts
description: Create one self-contained review folder for every attempted local SVG playback, including failed or cancelled runs. Preserve exact inputs, diagnostics, deterministic render script, generated player, reset outcomes, and a review-note template so subjective feedback can be attributed and compared.
acceptance_criteria: Each run has a stable ID and manifest, exact plan/profile copies, validation warnings, renderer-log.jsonl/render script, self-contained player.html, and review-notes.md. Logs distinguish planned events, resolved abstract controls, frames/gesture triggers, suppression/coalescing decisions, cancellation/errors, and final reset/release with event IDs and timestamps. Optional recording path/checksum can be attached. Incomplete runs are retained/marked. Tests cover deterministic successful, cancelled, and failed finalization; npm run check passes.
notes:
Implemented durable local SVG review bundles via npm run demo:run. Every finalized bundle contains an explicit offline AI-delegate manifest, exact-byte plan/profile copies and source SHA-256s, validation diagnostics, deterministic render-script.jsonl, enriched renderer-log.jsonl, matching self-contained player.html, and template-derived review-notes.md. Manifests record artifact checksums, outcome, reset/release evidence, and optional recording path/checksum fields. Run IDs are path-safe timestamp+content-hash identifiers and existing run folders are never overwritten.

Completed, cancelled, and explicit error outcomes finalize with matching scripts/players; malformed inputs retain exact sources plus diagnostics and a visibly failed player without fabricating a render script. Predictable adapter/profile generation failures retain any successfully compiled script and finalize as checksummed failed bundles. Unexpected finalization failures leave an incomplete manifest and generation-error evidence. Renderer logs distinguish validation, event edges, abstract fixed-tick frames, active event IDs/timestamps, resolved SVG controls, pose coalescing, later-pose-wins suppression, interruptions, final reset, and release.

Verification: npm run check (52 tests); npm run demo:dry; npm run demo:mapping; npm run demo:run. Chrome file:// verification of an injected cancelled run confirmed planned duration 28000ms, terminal duration 1450ms, no resource requests, visible Cancelled status, and exact neutral controls after the deterministic reset ramp. Independent review blockers around predictable-failure retention and duplicate coalescing logs were fixed before closure. No VTube dependency was used.
close_reason: Durable completed/cancelled/error/failed/incomplete local SVG run bundles, logs, checksums, tests, and cancelled-player verification are complete.

title: M1-007: Create the first restrained local SVG mannerism mapping
description: Tune the reference vocabulary on the first-party local SVG avatar after compiler/player playback exists. Optimize for calm legibility and authority boundaries at approximately 320px meeting-tile width, not motion density or decorative complexity. Keep semantic taste/easing in runtime and SVG identifiers in the profile/adapter.
acceptance_criteria: Listening, thinking, agreement, uncertainty/caveat, speaking-ready, deferral, boundary, and neutral reset are visually distinct. Motion remains within documented style limits and includes intentional stillness. Mapping constants are centralized/reviewable and no renderer IDs enter animation-plan JSON. A local-browser review at full and 320px size records remaining plan/runtime/rig/renderer defects.
notes:
Implemented the first restrained local SVG vocabulary. Runtime now maps semantic attention to renderer-neutral gaze controls; the rig profile centralizes stronger but bounded state/gesture targets; and the local SVG adapter centralizes visual scale constants plus explicit caveat, speaking-mouth, and handoff cues. Added regression coverage for distinct reference-state fingerprints, intentional stillness, boundary-vs-deferral, bounded targets, exact neutral reset, deterministic/self-contained output, and renderer-ID isolation.

Verification: npm run check (45 tests); npm run demo:dry; npm run demo:mapping; npm run --silent demo:render-script; npm run demo:player. Chrome file:// review passed at an exact 320px stage: listening gaze, thinking gaze/tilt, agreement smile, uncertainty/caveat mark, speaking-ready open mouth, deferral handoff label, and neutral reset were visually reviewed. Badge wrapping and handoff legibility were tuned after the first contact-sheet review. Start/countdown, pause/resume, restart, completion, exact neutral reset, and zero resource requests were rechecked. No VTube application or network dependency was used.

Remaining defects for later review: the intentionally near-neutral listening pose is most legible in motion rather than as a static thumbnail; the current abstract avatar is a review rig, not a David-specific asset. Safari evidence was not recorded.
close_reason: Restrained local SVG vocabulary implemented, covered, and manually reviewed in Chrome at 320px; VTube-independent path remains intact.

title: M1-006: Stream smoothed VTube parameter injection
description: Render persistent semantic state/posture as time-continuous VTube input values. The runtime owns interpolation, tick cadence, ownership, clamping, repeated refresh, cancellation, neutral hold, and final release so state changes do not jump or silently expire.
acceptance_criteria: Tick rate and easing have documented defaults and use a monotonic clock. Active values are resent often enough to retain control, clamped through rig min/max/neutral mappings, and have one set-mode owner per parameter. Start/end/cancel transitions do not jump or drift. End/error/cancellation holds neutral briefly then releases injection. Fake-clock/runtime tests cover interpolation and reset deterministically; npm run check and a real-model manual run pass.
notes: Deferred on 2026-07-14 because organizational IT policy blocks VTube Studio installation. See ADR 0005 once added. No VTube acceptance evidence was claimed; resume only if policy approval and a usable installation become available.
close_reason: Superseded by the deterministic easing/runtime compiler in aime-6gm.13 and reviewed local SVG mannerism mapping in aime-6gm.8. VTube parameter injection is no longer part of the current product direction; no VTube streaming evidence is claimed.

title: M1-004c: Implement complete model and rig preflight
description:
Add the fail-closed boundary between authenticated connection and playback. Preflight must inspect the entire loaded environment and resolve every rig selector before any event is scheduled, reporting all incompatibilities together rather than failing halfway through playback.

Non-goal: timed playback.
acceptance_criteria: A preflight command verifies API/authentication state and a loaded model; strict mode refuses an unbound or mismatched rig profile. Current-model hotkeys are listed with name, unique ID, type, and backing file where available. Duplicate names are rejected unless the profile uses a unique ID; all selectors resolve to unique IDs. Available VTube input parameters are checked against every rig mapping. One typed/human-readable report identifies all missing or ambiguous controls with meaningful exit status. Automated fixture tests and a real macOS run pass, and no animation command is sent.
notes: Deferred on 2026-07-14 because organizational IT policy blocks VTube Studio installation. See ADR 0005 once added. No VTube acceptance evidence was claimed; resume only if policy approval and a usable installation become available.
close_reason: Superseded by the completed local_svg profile validation and bounded mapping in aime-6gm.12 plus durable diagnostics in aime-6gm.9. VTube model/session preflight is no longer required; no VTube preflight evidence is claimed.

title: M1-005: Schedule and execute preflighted hotkeys
description: Implement deterministic discrete gesture/expression playback from validated plan events and preflight-resolved hotkey IDs. The runtime, not the planner, owns the monotonic clock, ordering, countdown, cancellation, conflict handling, lifecycle, and reset behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Playback uses a monotonic clock and optional recording countdown. Same-time events execute in deterministic order. Only preflighted unique hotkey IDs are used. Duplicate commands are rejected or deliberately coalesced; toggle-expression activation/deactivation is explicit and reset is idempotent. Scheduled, sent, acknowledged, failed, and queue/cooldown outcomes are logged. Cancellation prevents future events. Normal completion, cancellation, and recoverable errors attempt reset-neutral. Deterministic fake-clock tests, npm run check, and a real-model manual run pass.
notes: Deferred on 2026-07-14 because organizational IT policy blocks VTube Studio installation. See ADR 0005 once added. No VTube acceptance evidence was claimed; resume only if policy approval and a usable installation become available.
close_reason: Superseded by the deterministic renderer-neutral scheduler in aime-6gm.13 and completed local SVG player in aime-6gm.14. VTube hotkey execution is no longer part of the current product direction; no VTube playback is claimed.

title: M1-004b: Implement token persistence and authenticated VTube session
description:
Build the reusable authenticated session on top of the selected production client. The first run should request user approval once, persist the token outside version control, and later runs should authenticate without another prompt. Failure states must be distinguishable and actionable.

Non-goal: inspect rig mappings or send playback commands.
acceptance_criteria: Host and port are configurable with localhost:8001 defaults. Stable plugin name/developer values are 3–32 characters and validated before token requests. Token storage is ignored, private where practical, and bound to plugin metadata. First authorization, token reuse after restart, denied approval, invalid/revoked token reauthorization, disabled API, unreachable endpoint, close, and reconnect behavior are covered by automated tests and a documented macOS manual run. No animation command is sent.
notes: Deferred on 2026-07-14 because organizational IT policy blocks VTube Studio installation. See ADR 0005 once added. No VTube acceptance evidence was claimed; resume only if policy approval and a usable installation become available.
close_reason: Superseded by the completed offline local SVG player in aime-6gm.14, which requires no external session, authentication, or token persistence. No VTube authentication behavior is claimed.

title: M1-004a: Decide and integrate the production VTube API client
description:
## Decision Needed

Choose the single production VTube API client before adding session behavior. Compare the current thin ws client with pinned vtubestudio@3.11.0 using the narrow live probe: connect, authenticate, inspect model/hotkeys/inputs, retain request/error context, close/reconnect predictably, and expose a logging seam.

Current recommendation: adopt VTubeStudioJS behind a narrow project adapter, override its aggressive timeout/readiness behavior, and retire or clearly demote the duplicate protocol implementation. The decision remains pending until the live probe records evidence.

## Rationale

Authentication, token reuse, typed endpoint responses, and reconnect behavior are the complex missing pieces. VTubeStudioJS already supplies them, while a local adapter can preserve project-owned readiness gates, timeouts, diagnostics, logging, and renderer-session semantics. Exactly one production path avoids protocol and error-handling drift.

## Alternatives Considered

1. Extend the in-repo thin client: maximizes lifecycle and logging control but makes this project maintain authentication, reconnect, response types, events, and API evolution.
2. Adopt VTubeStudioJS behind a local adapter: reduces protocol maintenance but requires tests around its constructor-driven connection, one-second default timeout, fixed reconnect loop, and terminal disconnect behavior.
3. Keep both clients: rejected because duplicate production paths would create semantic drift and double the test/maintenance burden.

## Non-Goals

Do not implement timed playback, parameter easing, or run artifacts in this decision issue.
acceptance_criteria: ADR 0004 records evidence and an accepted decision. Exactly one production client remains; any dependency is pinned intentionally. The project adapter exposes explicit authenticated start/close lifecycle, configurable endpoint, project-owned timeout/error mapping, and raw request/result logging seams. Reconnect is treated as a new renderer session. Adapter/protocol tests pass, npm run check passes, and a documented live probe confirms the selected client against the generic model.
notes: Deferred on 2026-07-14 because organizational IT policy blocks VTube Studio installation. See ADR 0005 once added. No VTube acceptance evidence was claimed; resume only if policy approval and a usable installation become available.
close_reason: Superseded by the accepted local SVG renderer decision in aime-6gm.11 and completed self-contained player in aime-6gm.14. A production VTube API client is no longer relevant to the current product direction; no VTube integration is claimed.

title: M1-000: Publish a reproducible public repository baseline
description:
Establish the reviewable source-control and issue-tracking baseline before renderer work begins. Publish the existing scaffold without private assets or credentials, make dependency installation registry-portable, and make the Beads graph durable through the repository remote.

Non-goal: change controller behavior.
acceptance_criteria: dlg0/ai-me is public with main as the default branch; commits use the GitHub noreply identity; ignored token/private-asset paths and generated output are not published; package-lock.json contains no environment-specific registry URLs; npm ci, npm run check, and npm run demo:dry pass; Beads hooks/configuration and the exported issue graph are present; refs/dolt/data is pushed; GitHub Actions completes successfully.
close_reason: Public dlg0/ai-me repository created; noreply identity and conservative public license set; lockfile normalized; npm ci/check/demo:dry pass; Beads graph and refs/dolt/data pushed; GitHub Actions passed.

title: Prepare a known-good VTube test model and bound rig profile
description:
Provide the external test fixture needed to validate the real VTube boundary without waiting for the David-specific avatar. Load a known-good Live2D model, enable Plugin API access, identify its model ID/name, inputs, and hotkeys, and produce a versioned rig profile bound to that installed model.

Keep licensed model binaries and local VTube state outside Git. The David-specific artwork/rig remains a parallel asset track and is not a prerequisite.
acceptance_criteria: VTube Studio is running on macOS with Plugin API access enabled and one known-good model loaded. A committed rig-profile.v1 fixture binds expectedModelId or exact expectedModelName, maps every control used by the reference plan, and records selectors that can be resolved by preflight. Manual setup and reset-neutral checks are documented. No licensed model binary, token, or private asset is committed.
notes:
Repository-side setup preparation completed on 2026-07-14. docs/LOCAL_SETUP_MACOS.md now documents the official Steam installation path, bundled example-model fixture policy, API-authoritative model/hotkey/input discovery, bound-profile workflow, and idempotent reset checks. npm run check passes (16/16). External blocker: VTube Studio and Steam are not installed on this Mac, no model is available, and no process listens on TCP 8001. Human action required: install/launch official VTube Studio, accept prompts, load a bundled example model, enable Allow Plugin API access, and leave it running.
Deferred on 2026-07-14 because organizational IT policy blocks VTube Studio installation. See ADR 0005 once added. No VTube acceptance evidence was claimed; resume only if policy approval and a usable installation become available.
close_reason: Superseded by the completed first-party local SVG rig/profile path in aime-6gm.12 and reviewed playback in aime-6gm.10. A VTube test model is no longer required for the current product direction; no VTube evidence is claimed.

title: Milestone 1: Reviewable offline delegate animation
description:
Deliver the smallest end-to-end proof of the project hypothesis under organizational policy constraints: a validated semantic animation plan drives a deterministic first-party local SVG avatar, resets safely, and leaves a replayable review bundle.

Scope completed: local SVG rig profile, deterministic scheduler/easing compiler, self-contained offline player, restrained mapping, durable artifacts, and the first reviewed clip. The six former VTube children are closed as superseded by this alternative, not implemented.

Non-goals: proprietary Live2D browser runtimes, policy bypass, LLM provider integration, TTS/lip sync, OBS/Teams, transcription, RAG, photorealism, or a polished UI.
acceptance_criteria: A 20–45 second reference plan animates the local SVG avatar and visibly communicates listening, thinking, mild agreement, uncertainty/caveat, qualified-answer posture, deferral, and neutral reset at approximately 320px width. Every run preserves plan/profile, diagnostics, deterministic render script, player, review notes, and optional recording reference. Full-duration AI-delegate disclosure is rendered. npm run check, demo:dry, mapping inspection, deterministic replay checks, and local-browser manual review pass.
close_reason: Milestone 1 is complete with all 14 child issues closed. Eight policy-safe local SVG/baseline issues were implemented and verified. Six VTube model/client/session/preflight/hotkey/parameter issues were closed as superseded—not implemented—because the completed local SVG profile, renderer-neutral runtime, self-contained player, durable artifacts, and comparable review provide the approved alternative. No VTube evidence is claimed, and any future external renderer requires a fresh decision and newly scoped issues.
